trigger: parser type mapping for Copilot/Cursor/Continue
options: A: separate json parser type
B: reuse jsonl parser type as design doc says JSON can be adapted by JsonlParser
rationale: Design doc FR-008 explicitly states JSON format (Copilot, Cursor, Continue) can be adapted by JsonlParser, so parserType='jsonl' is correct

trigger: Amp yoloFlag value
options: A: empty string (Amp has built-in auto mode)
B: some flag like --auto
rationale: Design doc says Amp YOLO mode is '内置' (built-in), so empty string is correct

trigger: StreamJsonParser event type mapping
options: A: strict mapping only known types
B: map known types + fallback unknown with content to text
rationale: Design doc says extract text/tool_use/error events, fallback for unknown types with content field is safer for forward compatibility
residual_risk: low - unknown event types become text

trigger: TextStreamParser error detection strategy
options: A: regex patterns for common error prefixes
B: single generic error regex
rationale: FR-013 says extract error info from text; multiple patterns (Error/fatal/exception/traceback/panic/FAIL) cover CLI tools like Aider, Amazon Q, Goose
residual_risk: low - may miss unusual error formats but covers common cases

trigger: stream interruption handling
options: A: let error propagate, lose buffered data
B: catch read error, flush buffers, then rethrow
rationale: AC-5 requires preserving parsed content when stream breaks; TextStreamParser catches read errors to flush text/code buffers before rethrowing
residual_risk: none - all three parsers preserve data on interruption

trigger: ProcessManager extends EventEmitter vs custom event system
options: A: custom event callback system
B: extend Node.js EventEmitter
rationale: EventEmitter is the standard Node.js pattern for event-driven classes, provides well-tested on/off/emit API, consistent with child_process conventions

trigger: env inheritance strategy for child processes
options: A: only pass opts.env (no parent env)
B: merge parent env with opts.env overrides
rationale: FR-012 says 'independent env' meaning custom vars, but CLI tools need PATH and other system vars to run; merging parent env with overrides is the practical approach
residual_risk: low - parent env vars are inherited but can be overridden

trigger: heartbeat options as separate parameter vs embedded in ExecOptions
options: A: add heartbeat fields to ExecOptions type
B: separate HeartbeatOptions parameter
rationale: ExecOptions is a shared type used by CLIAdapter interface; heartbeat is ProcessManager-specific concern, separate parameter avoids polluting shared type

trigger: execute() signature with optional sessionOpts parameter
options: A: add sessionOpts to ExecOptions type
B: add optional third parameter to execute method
rationale: CLIAdapter interface defines execute(prompt, opts), adding optional third param is TS-compatible without modifying shared interface; sessionOpts is Claude-Code-specific concern
residual_risk: none - optional param is backward compatible

trigger: env isolation strategy - copy vs reference
options: A: shallow copy process.env then delete CLAUDECODE
B: spread process.env into new object then delete CLAUDECODE
rationale: FR-009 AC-034 requires env isolation without affecting host process; spread creates independent copy, delete on copy doesn't touch process.env
residual_risk: none - host process.env unchanged, verified by test

trigger: stdout Node.js Readable to Web ReadableStream conversion
options: A: use Node.js stream.Readable.toWeb()
B: manual ReadableStream wrapper with on('data')/on('end')
rationale: Manual wrapper is more explicit and works across Node.js versions; toWeb() requires Node 17+ and may have edge cases with child_process streams

trigger: reviewer mode args: include prompt or not
options: A: codex review <prompt> --json
B: codex review --json (no prompt, review is implicit)
rationale: Design doc FR-010 says 'codex review' is a natural fit for Reviewer role, review command inspects current repo state without needing a prompt argument

trigger: git repo check implementation strategy
options: A: use fs.existsSync('.git')
B: use execFile('git', ['rev-parse', '--is-inside-work-tree'])
rationale: git rev-parse handles nested repos and worktrees correctly; .git check only works for top-level repos
residual_risk: low - adds ~50ms startup latency for git check

trigger: git warning delivery mechanism
options: A: console.warn before execute
B: yield a status OutputChunk as first item in stream
rationale: Using OutputChunk stream keeps warning visible in TUI and consistent with adapter output contract; console.warn may be lost in TUI context

trigger: GeminiAdapter simplicity - no session options needed
options: A: add GeminiSessionOptions like ClaudeCode/Codex
B: no session options, simple execute(prompt, opts)
rationale: Design doc FR-011 specifies simple invocation: gemini -p <prompt> --output-format stream-json --non-interactive --yolo. No session recovery or role switching features mentioned. Keeping API minimal.

trigger: multi-consumer broadcast pattern: EventEmitter vs push-based queue
options: A: EventEmitter with on('chunk') listeners
B: push-based consumer queues with async iterator protocol
rationale: Push-based queues provide natural backpressure via async iterator protocol, each consumer gets its own queue with Promise-based signaling; EventEmitter would require wrapping in async iterator anyway

trigger: getBufferedText join strategy
options: A: join with empty string (concatenate raw)
B: join with space separator
rationale: Space separator provides readable text output for context passing; chunks may not have trailing spaces. Consumers needing raw concatenation can use getBuffer() and join manually
residual_risk: low - extra spaces between chunks, acceptable for context summarization

trigger: interrupt mechanism: AbortController vs boolean flag
options: A: use AbortController/AbortSignal
B: simple boolean flag checked in pump loop
rationale: Boolean flag is simpler and sufficient since pump loop is the only consumer of the signal; AbortController adds complexity without benefit for internal-only use

trigger: token estimation strategy
options: A: use tiktoken for accurate token count
B: approximate 1 token ≈ 4 chars
rationale: Fallback truncation strategy per spec; tiktoken adds heavy dependency for v1. Approximation is sufficient for budget enforcement
residual_risk: low - may over/under-estimate by ~20% for non-English text

trigger: template variable syntax
options: A: {{variable}} mustache-style
B: ${variable} template literal style
rationale: Mustache-style avoids conflicts with JS template literals and is more familiar for non-programmers editing .duo/prompts/ files

trigger: summary generation: LLM call vs truncation fallback
options: A: implement LLM-based summary now
B: truncation fallback only, LLM summary deferred
rationale: Design doc says 'or use simple truncation as fallback'; LLM summary requires adapter integration which is a cross-cutting concern better handled in a later card
residual_risk: low - summaries are less informative than LLM-generated ones but functional

trigger: CLI arg parsing approach: minimist/commander vs manual
options: A: use minimist or commander dependency
B: manual argv parsing with switch statement
rationale: Only 4 simple flags needed (--dir, --coder, --reviewer, --task); adding a dependency for this is over-engineering. Manual parsing is straightforward and dependency-free

trigger: cli-commands.ts separation from cli.ts
options: A: put handleStart logic in cli.ts
B: separate cli-commands.ts for testability
rationale: cli.ts is the entry point with process.argv/process.exit side effects; separating command handlers into cli-commands.ts makes them unit-testable with injectable log function

trigger: interactive mode: full TUI vs deferred
options: A: implement full interactive TUI prompts now
B: detect missing args and signal needsInteractive flag
rationale: FR-019 project directory selector and TUI components are separate cards (Phase E/F). Card C.2 scope is 'duo start' command with arg parsing and validation. Interactive TUI will build on top of the needsInteractive signal
residual_risk: low - interactive mode not yet functional, but CLI arg mode is complete

trigger: session ID generation strategy
options: A: crypto.randomUUID()
B: date-based prefix + short random
rationale: crypto.randomUUID() is standard, collision-free, and doesn't require custom formatting logic

trigger: session file format: JSON pretty vs compact
options: A: compact JSON (single line)
B: pretty-printed JSON with 2-space indent
rationale: Pretty-printed makes session files human-readable for debugging; file size is negligible for session metadata

trigger: handleResume/handleResumeList as sync vs async
options: A: async functions
B: sync functions (only fs sync ops)
rationale: SessionManager uses only synchronous fs operations (readFileSync, writeFileSync, existsSync); no async I/O needed, keeping API simple

trigger: xstate v5 context initialization strategy
options: A: use machine context directly with defaults
B: use input factory pattern for context initialization
rationale: xstate v5 recommends input factory for dynamic context; enables custom maxRounds/round via createActor input while keeping defaults

trigger: guard implementation for NOT_CONVERGED branching
options: A: single transition with conditional target
B: array of transitions with guard conditions
rationale: xstate v5 array transition pattern is idiomatic; first matching guard wins, fallback (no guard) handles maxRounds reached case

trigger: routing events naming
options: A: use ROUTE_TO_REVIEW, ROUTE_TO_CODER, etc.
B: use choice_detected_for_user, route_to_review as in spec diagram
rationale: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE is consistent with all other events (START_TASK, CODE_COMPLETE etc.); spec diagram labels are descriptions not event names

trigger: LLM classification vs keyword+regex fallback
options: A: implement LLM (Haiku) classification now
B: keyword+regex fallback, defer LLM
rationale: AC-016 says 'use lightweight LLM OR keyword+regex fallback'; v1 uses regex for zero external dependency, LLM can be added later
residual_risk: low - regex may misclassify ambiguous outputs but covers common patterns

trigger: loop detection algorithm
options: A: exact string match
B: Jaccard keyword similarity with threshold
rationale: AC-019 says 'same feedback topic' not 'same text'; Jaccard similarity on extracted keywords detects semantically similar outputs that aren't identical
residual_risk: low - threshold 0.35 may miss very different phrasings of same issue

trigger: question detection: strict question mark only vs question mark + choice-intro phrases
options: A: only detect lines ending with ?/？
B: also detect lines with choice-introducing phrases (options/choose/prefer/which)
rationale: Numbered list after 'I see a few options:' is a common choice pattern; requiring strict question mark would miss valid cases
residual_risk: low - CHOICE_INTRO_LINE patterns are conservative, requires both intro phrase AND 2+ list items

trigger: forward prompt language
options: A: Chinese-only forward prompt
B: English forward prompt with task context
rationale: LLM tools are primarily English-based; English forward prompt is universally understood by all supported CLI tools

trigger: double Ctrl+C detection: timestamp diff vs flag-based
options: A: timestamp diff > 0 check
B: hasPendingSigint boolean flag + threshold check
rationale: Date.now() can return same value for rapid sequential calls; boolean flag reliably distinguishes first vs subsequent sigint regardless of timing granularity

trigger: InterruptHandler architecture: dependency injection vs direct imports
options: A: direct import ProcessManager/SessionManager
B: dependency injection via InterruptHandlerDeps interface
rationale: DI enables pure unit testing with mocks; InterruptHandler doesn't own ProcessManager/SessionManager lifecycle, just coordinates them

trigger: active state detection for interrupt eligibility
options: A: check processManager.isRunning() only
B: check actor state is in ACTIVE_STATES set (CODING/REVIEWING)
rationale: State machine is the source of truth for workflow state; process might be running during transitions but interrupt should only apply in LLM-active states per FR-007

trigger: scrolling unit: per-message vs per-line
options: A: scroll by individual text lines
B: scroll by message (each message as one unit)
rationale: Messages are the natural unit in a chat-style UI; per-line scrolling within messages is deferred to E-2 streaming render. Per-message scroll is simpler and matches AC-049 'j/k scrolling'
residual_risk: low - very long single messages may need internal scrolling, handled in E-2

trigger: Gemini orange color representation
options: A: use 'yellow' (closest ANSI basic color)
B: use hex '#FFA500' for true orange
rationale: Ink supports hex colors via chalk; hex provides accurate orange vs yellow which is already used for System role. Color+shape dual encoding (AC-050) requires distinct colors per role
residual_risk: none - terminals without truecolor will fallback gracefully

trigger: layout height allocation strategy
options: A: fixed pixel allocation
B: dynamic calculation from terminal rows
rationale: Terminal size varies; dynamic calculation (rows - statusBar - inputArea - separators) ensures message area fills available space. Props-based columns/rows allow testing with fixed dimensions

trigger: markdown parsing architecture: regex-based vs AST library
options: A: use remark/unified AST library
B: lightweight regex-based parser
rationale: Only need code blocks, lists, bold/italic, tables for TUI; full AST adds heavy dependency for simple rendering needs. Regex parser is ~120 lines and sufficient
residual_risk: low - may miss edge cases in deeply nested markdown but covers LLM output patterns

trigger: streaming indicator: animated timer vs static spinner char
options: A: setInterval-based animation cycling through spinner chars
B: deterministic spinner char based on content length
rationale: Ink re-renders on state change; content updates during streaming naturally cycle the spinner. Avoids timer management and test complexity with fake timers
residual_risk: none - spinner visually changes as content grows during streaming

trigger: MessageView integration: wrapper vs inline rendering
options: A: inline markdown parsing in MessageView
B: delegate to StreamRenderer component
rationale: Separation of concerns: MessageView handles layout/role styling, StreamRenderer handles content rendering with markdown. StreamRenderer is reusable for other contexts

trigger: input handling testability: useInput in ink-testing-library unreliable
options: A: test via stdin.write interaction only
B: extract processInput pure function + render tests
rationale: ink-testing-library v4 + ink v6 stdin.write doesn't reliably trigger useInput callbacks; extracting pure processInput function enables thorough unit testing of all input logic
residual_risk: none - pure function tests cover all logic, smoke tests cover integration

trigger: multiline rendering: single Text with newlines vs separate Box per line
options: A: single Text element with embedded newlines
B: first line in prompt row + extra Box per additional line
rationale: Separate Box per line gives better control over indentation (continuation lines use 2-space indent vs '> ' for first line) and height calculation

trigger: MainLayout InputArea integration: required vs optional props
options: A: make isLLMRunning and onInputSubmit required on MainLayout
B: make them optional with defaults
rationale: Backward compatibility with existing MainLayout tests and callers; defaults (isLLMRunning=false, onInputSubmit=noop) are safe

trigger: StatusBar as standalone component vs MainLayout refactor
options: A: refactor MainLayout to accept StatusBarProps instead of statusText
B: keep StatusBar as standalone exportable component, preserve MainLayout API
rationale: Modifying MainLayout interface would break E.1 tests and existing callers; StatusBar is independently composable and can replace statusText rendering at integration time

trigger: token display format
options: A: always show raw number
B: show raw for <1000, N.Nk for >=1000
rationale: Compact format fits 1-line status bar constraint; 25000 as '25.0k' is more readable in limited space

trigger: spinner animation implementation
options: A: setInterval cycling spinner chars
B: icon-only (◆) without animation, rely on Ink re-renders during streaming
rationale: StatusBar receives status prop from parent; ◆ green icon indicates active state. True animation requires timer in parent or useEffect, adding complexity for v1; icon is sufficient per AC-062
residual_risk: low - no character cycling animation, but green ◆ clearly indicates LLM active state

trigger: CodeBlock state management: internal useState vs controlled props
options: A: internal useState with uncontrolled component
B: controlled expanded/onToggle props with parent state in StreamRenderer
rationale: Controlled props enable StreamRenderer to manage expand/collapse state via React state map, ensuring persistence across re-renders (AC-054 scroll state persistence). Parent owns the state, CodeBlock is a pure presentational component

trigger: code block rendering delegation in StreamRenderer
options: A: keep code_block in SegmentView, pass state via context
B: handle code_block directly in StreamRenderer render loop
rationale: Direct handling in render loop gives natural access to expandedBlocks state map and codeBlockIndex counter; avoids React context overhead for simple state

trigger: scroll state testability: useInput untestable in ink-testing-library
options: A: test via stdin.write interaction only
B: extract pure scroll state functions + render tests
rationale: ink-testing-library v4 stdin.write doesn't reliably trigger useInput callbacks; extracting pure functions (scrollUp/scrollDown/jumpToEnd/computeScrollView) enables thorough unit testing of all scroll logic, same pattern as E.3 InputArea processInput extraction
residual_risk: none - pure function tests cover all scroll logic, integration test verifies rendering

trigger: new output detection: useEffect vs synchronous ref tracking vs state-based lockedAtCount
options: A: useEffect with prevMessageCountRef
B: synchronous ref mutation during render
C: pure state field lockedAtCount in ScrollState
rationale: useEffect doesn't flush synchronously in ink-testing-library making it untestable; ref mutation during render is a React anti-pattern in strict mode; pure state field in ScrollState makes detection deterministic and testable as pure function

trigger: indicator space reservation strategy
options: A: overlay indicator on top of last message
B: reduce visibleSlots by 1 when indicator visible
rationale: Reducing visible slots ensures indicator is never clipped by overflow:hidden; overlay approach would require absolute positioning which Ink doesn't support well

trigger: round summary representation: special Message vs separate component
options: A: special Message with isRoundSummary metadata flag
B: separate component rendered between message groups
rationale: Message-based approach is compatible with existing messages[] array, scroll logic, and filterMessages; no MainLayout render loop changes needed

trigger: display mode state location
options: A: MainLayout internal useState only
B: pure function module + MainLayout integration
rationale: Pure functions (toggleDisplayMode, filterMessages) enable isolated unit testing, consistent with E.3 InputArea processInput and F.2 scroll-state patterns

trigger: verbose metadata on Message type: multiple fields vs single metadata object
options: A: separate optional fields (cliCommand, tokenCount, isRoutingEvent)
B: single metadata? object containing all verbose fields
rationale: Single metadata object keeps Message interface clean; all verbose/filtering fields are grouped together under MessageMetadata

trigger: testability pattern: vi.mock vs vi.spyOn for node:fs
options: A: vi.spyOn on fs functions
B: vi.mock('node:fs') at module level
rationale: ESM modules have non-configurable exports; vi.spyOn fails with 'Cannot redefine property'. vi.mock at module level is the standard vitest ESM pattern, consistent with detect.test.ts

trigger: component architecture: pure logic extraction vs inline in component
options: A: inline all logic in DirectoryPicker.tsx
B: extract pure functions to directory-picker-state.ts
rationale: Same pattern as InputArea processInput and scroll-state; pure functions enable thorough unit testing without ink-testing-library limitations

trigger: MRU file location
options: A: project-local .duo/recent.json
B: global ~/.duo/recent.json
rationale: AC-066 explicitly specifies '~/.duo/recent.json'; MRU is cross-project by nature - tracks which projects user has visited

trigger: keybinding context-dependent routing for ? and / keys
options: A: lift InputArea state to MainLayout fully
B: add special action type to processInput for ? and / when empty
rationale: Adding 'special' action type to InputAction is minimal change; InputArea calls onSpecialKey callback, MainLayout handles overlay opening. No need to fully lift state - controlled mode via value/onValueChange props is sufficient

trigger: overlay rendering: full-screen replacement vs z-index overlay
options: A: render overlay alongside normal layout with z-index
B: conditional rendering - overlay replaces normal layout
rationale: Ink doesn't support z-index or absolute positioning; conditional rendering is the standard Ink pattern for modal/overlay UIs. When overlay is open, normal layout is hidden entirely

trigger: Ctrl+L clear screen: delete messages vs hide via offset
options: A: mutate messages array (delete)
B: track clearedCount offset, slice filtered messages
rationale: FR-022 says 'clear screen, preserve history'; offset approach preserves all messages for search/scroll while hiding them from view. No data loss

trigger: search overlay input handling: separate useInput vs reuse MainLayout
options: A: SearchOverlay has its own useInput
B: MainLayout routes text input to search query when search overlay active
rationale: Centralizing input handling in MainLayout's useInput avoids multiple competing handlers; search query updates are simple character append/backspace operations

trigger: SystemMessage component architecture: single component with type prop vs separate components
options: A: three separate components (RoutingMessage, InterruptMessage, WaitingMessage)
B: single SystemMessage component with type prop dispatching to internal sub-components
rationale: Single entry point with type discriminator keeps import API clean; internal sub-components handle rendering. Consistent with design doc FR-024/FR-025 which describes these as system-level messages in the same message flow

trigger: ConvergenceCard/DisagreementCard architecture: single component with type prop vs separate components
options: A: single CardComponent with convergence/disagreement mode
B: separate ConvergenceCard and DisagreementCard components
rationale: Design doc FR-026 defines two distinct card layouts with different data shapes and action sets; separate components avoid conditional complexity and match spec diagrams exactly

trigger: git diff stats: runtime exec vs pure parser
options: A: parseGitDiffStat pure function parsing git diff --stat output
B: integrated function that spawns git process
rationale: Pure parser is testable without git; caller (workflow engine) is responsible for spawning git and passing output. Separation of concerns consistent with adapter pattern

trigger: CopilotAdapter and AiderAdapter implementation pattern
options: A: add adapter-specific session options like ClaudeCode/Codex
B: simple execute(prompt, opts) without session options
rationale: Registry shows copilot uses simple '-p <prompt> --allow-all-tools' and aider uses '-m <prompt> --yes-always'; no session recovery or role switching features needed for these tools, same pattern as GeminiAdapter

trigger: CopilotAdapter and AiderAdapter implementation pattern
options: A: add adapter-specific session options like ClaudeCode/Codex
B: simple execute(prompt, opts) without session options
rationale: Registry shows amp uses simple '-x <prompt>' with built-in auto mode, cline uses '-y <prompt> --json', qwen uses '-p <prompt> --output-format stream-json --yolo'; no session recovery or role switching features needed, same pattern as GeminiAdapter/CopilotAdapter

trigger: CursorAdapter, ContinueAdapter, AmazonQAdapter, GooseAdapter implementation pattern
options: A: add adapter-specific session options like ClaudeCode/Codex
B: simple execute(prompt, opts) without session options
rationale: Registry shows cursor uses 'cursor agent -p <prompt> --auto-approve', continue uses 'cn -p <prompt> --format json --allow', amazon-q uses 'q chat --no-interactive --trust-all-tools <prompt>', goose uses 'goose run -t <prompt>' with GOOSE_MODE=auto env; no session recovery or role switching features needed, same pattern as GeminiAdapter

trigger: GooseAdapter YOLO mode: env var vs CLI flag
options: A: pass GOOSE_MODE=auto as CLI arg prefix
B: set GOOSE_MODE=auto via env in ExecOptions
rationale: Registry yoloFlag='GOOSE_MODE=auto' is an env var, not a CLI flag; buildEnv() method merges with opts.env and passes to ProcessManager via ExecOptions.env
